evidence: Vrijen warned against the 'chicken or egg' problem and the risk of over-optimizing for a single stakeholder at the expense of broader product fit... avoid the 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots
seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is. A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options
He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly
Chris Hanson (special education teacher turned edtech founder)... building an ADHD support product called '360'
Both founders share similar backgrounds transitioning from non-technical fields into tech product development, with Vrijen validating Chris's accomplishment in building a polished product

source_display: AI as Brain Crutches for ADHD
evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as 'brain crutches' for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching
The core concept is a 'marketplace of all possible futures' - allowing users to 'jump into' career simulations mapped to real jobs and companies
Max used the metaphor of a 'neopet' - anytime you train or deploy your AI requires 'energy,' which is where they monetize through usage-based pricing
Vrijen connected this to the bet that AI agents with deeper understanding of the orchestrating individual will perform better than generic agents

source_display: Assessments Must Tie to Business Value
evidence: businesses only care whether you can add value to what they're making... passing an algorithm assessment doesn't prove someone can deliver value to the company

roles like product owner and project manager... exist primarily to organize attention but don't add direct value to products. Maximilian argued these roles are 'done' in the current AI-enabled landscape
engineers more than one step removed from the product were most vulnerable... visible links between individual work and the product/service being sold are increasingly essential in 2026
Vrijen observed that the market for novel hiring signals is small because snake oil assessment companies have burned goodwill in the past
Aim to sell Careerspan towards the middle of 2026, while using the next few months to pursue revenue generation opportunities to improve valuation
Maximilian (future simulation product founder)... preparing to launch his new product in approximately one month
He introduced context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses to generate desired outputs. He outlined three strategic levels
Three-level framework: Conversation (tactical), Environment (persistent context), Pipeline (external data integration) - mastering all three creates an emergent personal AI operating system

source_display: AI Productivity is a Mental Model Problem
evidence: Vrijen argued the core issue isn't a technology or tactics problem but a mental model problem—users collect techniques without understanding how they integrate into effective systems
Offensive and defensive prompting: Expansive generation (expand option set) vs. critical tightening (attack, break down, shore up)
Threshold rubrics: Ask the AI to create an evaluation rubric, fill it out, assign scores, and iterate output until achieving the highest level
Only 3,300 LinkedIn connections versus 45,000 imported contacts from a 2015 data import, revealing two largely disjoint professional networks
embrace the 'unhobbling' concept from the Anthropic/Claude team—recognizing the AI is capable of more than you initially realize

source_display: Plan Files as Future of Coding
evidence: documenting AI conversations in plan files that mix prose and code in stepwise fashion. He positioned this as the future of coding: planning systems through narrative structure rather than pure code alone
Ben Guo (co-founder of Zo Computer and Vrijen as a power Zo user)
metacognitive patterns for working effectively with AI: narrative before data, use yourself as a guinea pig, never accept the first answer, consistently ask 'what else?' and 'delight me'
Documented 1,800% increase in inbound connections when increasing posting frequency, strong evidence for visibility strategies
